name: BuySideFlow
alternateName: BuySideFlow: A Time-Anchored Benchmark for Executable SQL and SQL-Python Tasks in Chinese Buy-Side Investment Research
description: BuySideFlow is a time-anchored benchmark for executable SQL and SQL-Python tasks in Chinese buy-side investment research. The public audit package contains task statements, derived manifests, time-audit metadata, schema summaries where licensing permits, evaluator code, prompt assets, benchmark reports, tool-use logs, and provenance hashes. Full execution requires access to a fixed controlled snapshot of a licensed financial database.

license.name: Restricted research release
license.description: The public audit artifacts are released for research review and benchmark documentation. Licensed database rows, unrestricted query access, database credentials, and source documents are not redistributed. Full benchmark execution requires authorized access to the controlled database snapshot or use of the official evaluation service.

conditionsOfAccess: The public audit package is accessible without database credentials. Full execution is restricted to authorized users with access to the fixed licensed database snapshot, such as a read-only managed database instance under the data-provider license, or through an official evaluation service. Users may not export, redistribute, or reconstruct licensed database rows.
ethicsPolicy: Task statements are rewritten and anonymized from routine buy-side research workflows and public investment-research materials. The release excludes internal meeting notes, client information, account-specific holdings, raw licensed database rows, and unrestricted database credentials.
measurementTechnique: Executable SQL and SQL-Python workflow evaluation
Static point-in-time audit over reference SQL and Python
Provenance hashing for schema assets, prompt assets, reference artifacts, sidecars, evaluator code, judge cache, and database snapshot identifier
rai:dataCollection: Tasks were constructed from routine Chinese buy-side research workflows and public investment-research materials, then rewritten into standardized benchmark requests. The public package does not include proprietary source documents or licensed database rows.
rai:dataPreprocessing: Task text was anonymized and standardized. A generated manifest is rebuilt from task files, reference artifacts, time-audit results, provenance records, and a small override file for fields that cannot be reliably inferred.
rai:limitations: BuySideFlow is a license-aware private-database benchmark. The public audit package supports static inspection and reproducibility of metadata and provenance, but complete local execution requires authorized access to the fixed licensed database snapshot or an official evaluation service.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The public audit package is designed not to include personal client information, account-specific holdings, database credentials, internal meeting notes, or raw licensed financial rows.
rai:licenseAndUseTerms: Public audit artifacts may be used for research review and benchmark documentation. Licensed database rows and unrestricted query access are excluded and remain subject to the original data-provider license.

distribution.name: question_dataset_jsonl
distribution.description: Chinese task statements and instance identifiers.

distribution.name: manifest_generated_jsonl
distribution.description: Generated per-task metadata, including domain, level, execution mode, evaluation kind, time-audit status, output schema, reference hashes, sidecar hashes, and row/column counts.

distribution.name: manifest_overrides_json
distribution.description: Small manual override file for fields that cannot be reliably inferred, such as task level, necessary as-of-date clarification, empty-result explanation, and future-label reason.

distribution.name: dataset_card
distribution.description: Human-readable dataset card describing task construction, time anchoring, leakage guards, evaluation metrics, provenance, release boundary, and known limitations.

distribution.name: reference_table_usage
distribution.description: Reference-program table usage summary over the documented schema catalog.

distribution.name: gildata_table_catalog_reference_marked
distribution.description: Table catalog with reference-use markings. Public release is subject to schema licensing constraints.

distribution.name: run_eval_py
distribution.description: Offline evaluator entry point for executing submissions, comparing outputs, exporting benchmark reports, and writing per-task tool-use CSV files.

distribution.name: run_time_audit_py
distribution.description: Static time-audit entry point for reference SQL and Python files.

distribution.name: business_background
distribution.description: Controlled business-background prompt asset used by baseline agents.

distribution.name: fund_business_rules
distribution.description: Fund-domain business rules prompt asset used by baseline agents.

distribution.name: selection_guidance
distribution.description: Table-selection and schema-grounding guidance prompt asset used by baseline agents.

recordSet.name: questions
recordSet.description: One record per benchmark task statement.

field.name: instance_id
field.description: Unique task identifier such as stock_001, fund_042, bond_065, or macro_016.

field.name: instruction
field.description: Chinese buy-side research request presented to the model.

recordSet.name: manifest
recordSet.description: Generated per-task metadata used for audit, reporting, and paper statistics. The manifest is not the evaluation source of truth.

field.name: domain
field.description: Research domain: stock, fund, bond, or macro.

field.name: level
field.description: Task-complexity level: L1_retrieval, L2_sql_aggregation, L3_sql_python_analytics, or L4_multimodal_or_report.

field.name: mode
field.description: Evaluator execution-mode label. In this release, sql+python denotes SQL-centered tasks executed through a Python wrapper, while python denotes Python-mediated analytical or artifact workflows.

field.name: evaluation_kind
field.description: Output evaluation kind: csv, mixed, or vision.

field.name: time_anchor
field.description: Structured, extracted, or derived time anchor used for audit and reporting.

field.name: anchor_source
field.description: Source of the time anchor: inputs, instruction, instruction_derived, or instruction_year.

field.name: output_schema
field.description: Expected output columns or artifact schema derived from reference outputs.

field.name: runtime_date_function
field.description: Reference audit status for prohibited runtime date functions.

field.name: financial_publish_guard
field.description: Reference audit status for financial disclosure-date guards such as INFOPUBLDATE <= as_of_date.

field.name: snapshot_guard
field.description: Reference audit status for status-window guards such as EFFECTIVEDATE, CANCELDATE, LISTEDDATE, IMPLEMENTDATE, and REMOVEDATE relative to the observation date.

field.name: future_label_allowed
field.description: Whether future realized values are allowed as offline labels rather than point-in-time inputs.

field.name: reference_hash
field.description: Per-task SHA256 aggregate hash of reference programs and artifacts.

field.name: sidecar_hash
field.description: Per-task SHA256 aggregate hash of sidecar files, or the empty hash when no sidecar exists.
